revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Amatsu-jinja (Itoigawa)]][[th:ศาลเจ้าอามัตสึ]] [[en:Amatsu Shrine]] [[fr:Amatsu-jinja]] [[ja:天津神社 (糸魚川市)]] {{Expand Japanese|天津神社 (糸魚川市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Amatsu Shrine (Q172253) | native_name = 天津神社 | image = Amatsu-jinja (Itoigawa), haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Amatsu Shrine (Q172253) | coordinates = {{coord|37.04013889|137.86425}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|de|Ninigi|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|eo|Ninigi|es|Ninigi no Mikoto|fa|نینیگی-نو-میکوتو|fr|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|id|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|it|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギ|ko|니니기|mni|ꯅꯤꯅꯤꯒꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|nl|Ninigi|no|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|pl|Ninigi|pt|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ru|Ниниги но Микото|simple|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|sv|Ninigi|th|นินิงิ โนะ มิโกโตะ|tl|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|tr|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|uk|Нініґі но мікото|vi|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|zh|瓊瓊杵尊|qid=Q1056017|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Amatsu Shrine'''|天津神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Echigo Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Ichinomiya|lt=ichinomiya|ca|Ichinomiya (Xinto)|de|Ichi-no-miya|en|Ichinomiya|fr|Ichi-no-miya|id|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|ko|이치노미야|pt|Ichinomiya (templo)|simple|Ichinomiya|th|อิจิโนมิยะ|uk|Ітіномія (титул)|zh|一宮|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} ** P1001: {{ill|Echigo Province|lt=Echigo Province|be|Этыго (правінцыя)|ckb|پارێزگای ئێچیگۆ|cs|Provincie Ečigo|de|Provinz Echigo|en|Echigo Province|es|Provincia de Echigo|fa|ولایت اچیگو|fr|Province d'Echigo|gl|Provincia de Echigo|id|Provinsi Echigo|it|Echigo|ja|越後国|ko|에치고국|lt|Ečigo žemė|nl|Echigo (provincie)|pt|Província de Echigo|ru|Этиго (провинция)|simple|Echigo Province|sk|Ečigo (provincia)|sr|Провинција Ечиго|tr|Echigo (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Етіґо|zh_min_nan|Etigo-kok|zh_yue|越後國|zh|越後國|qid=Q666514|1=Echigo Province}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database (Q135159299) https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182420 * {{ill|Q135419779|ltq=Q135419779|qid=Q135419779|1=Shikinai Supershrine}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Echigo Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Echigo Province|ja|越後国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11636502|1=List of Shikinaisha in Echigo Province}} ** Series ordinal: 2 ** P155: {{ill|Nunakahano Shrine|lt=Nunakahano Shrine|qid=Q135040414|1=Nunakahano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135040419|ltq=Q135040419|qid=Q135040419|1=Ahitano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Itoigawa|lt=Itoigawa|ar|إتويغاوا (نييغاتا)|azb|ایتوییقاوا، نیقاتا|ceb|Itoigawa (lungsod sa Hapon)|ce|Итоигава|de|Itoigawa|en|Itoigawa|envoyage|Itoigawa|es|Itoigawa|fa|ایتویی‌گاوا، نیگاتا|fi|Itoigawa|fr|Itoigawa|gl|Itoigawa|id|Itoigawa, Niigata|it|Itoigawa|ja|糸魚川市|ko|이토이가와시|mg|Itoigawa|ms|Itoigawa, Niigata|mzn|ایتویی‌گاوا، نیگاتا|nl|Itoigawa|pl|Itoigawa|pt|Itoigawa|ro|Itoigawa|ru|Итоигава|sv|Itoigawa|tg|Итоигава|tl|Itoigawa, Niigata|tr|Itoigawa|tt|Итоигава|uk|Ітоїґава|uz|Itoigava|vi|Itoigawa, Niigata|war|Itoigawa, Niigata|zh_classical|絲魚川市|zh_min_nan|Itoigawa-chhī|zh_yue|絲魚川|zh|糸魚川市|zhvoyage|糸魚川|qid=Q857208|1=Itoigawa}} * {{ill|Echigo Province|lt=Echigo Province|be|Этыго (правінцыя)|ckb|پارێزگای ئێچیگۆ|cs|Provincie Ečigo|de|Provinz Echigo|en|Echigo Province|es|Provincia de Echigo|fa|ولایت اچیگو|fr|Province d'Echigo|gl|Provincia de Echigo|id|Provinsi Echigo|it|Echigo|ja|越後国|ko|에치고국|lt|Ečigo žemė|nl|Echigo (provincie)|pt|Província de Echigo|ru|Этиго (провинция)|simple|Echigo Province|sk|Ečigo (provincia)|sr|Провинција Ечиго|tr|Echigo (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Етіґо|zh_min_nan|Etigo-kok|zh_yue|越後國|zh|越後國|qid=Q666514|1=Echigo Province}} * {{ill|Q11664982|ltq=Q11664982|qid=Q11664982|1=Kubiki district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|37.04013889|137.86425}} * {{coord|37.040177|137.864242}} Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Amatsu-jinja (Itoigawa), haiden.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|de|Ninigi|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|eo|Ninigi|es|Ninigi no Mikoto|fa|نینیگی-نو-میکوتو|fr|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|id|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|it|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|ニニギ|ko|니니기|mni|ꯅꯤꯅꯤꯒꯤ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|nl|Ninigi|no|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|pl|Ninigi|pt|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ru|Ниниги но Микото|simple|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|sv|Ninigi|th|นินิงิ โนะ มิโกโตะ|tl|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|tr|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|uk|Нініґі но мікото|vi|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|zh|瓊瓊杵尊|qid=Q1056017|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20160306025618/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/340201.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182420 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database (Q135159299) https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182420 * {{ill|Q11071127|ltq=Q11071127|qid=Q11071127|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database (Q135159299) https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182420 == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|Q7311343|ltq=Q7311343|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}}https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|lt=East Asian hip-and-gable roof|de|Irimoya|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|es|Tejado de gabletes de Asia oriental|fr|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|ko|팔작지붕|ru|Иримоя-дзукури|uk|Напіввальмовий дах|zh|歇山顶|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == postal code (P281) == * 941-0056https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 8317429 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7110005010811 == Commons category (P373) == * Amatsu-jinja (Itoigawa) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Ohomuwano Shrine|lt=Ohomuwano Shrine|qid=Q135040416|1=Ohomuwano Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Q135195027|ltq=Q135195027|qid=Q135195027|1=Nunagawa Shrine (Ama-no Shrine precinct)}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '新潟県糸魚川市一の宮1-3-34', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/0nb82lfFreebase Data Dumps == image of entrance (P9721) == * Amatsu-jinja (Itoigawa), torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Amatsu-jinja (Itoigawa)}} [[EN:Amatsu Shrine]] [[FR:Amatsu-jinja]] [[JA:天津神社 (糸魚川市)]] [[TH:ศาลเจ้าอามัตสึ]] {{wikidata link|Q172253}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Amatsu Shrine |Image = [[File:Amatsu-jinja (Itoigawa), haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|qid=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Itoigawa City|Q857208|qid=Q857208|1=Itoigawa}} Ichinomiya 1-3-34 |Coordinates = {{Wikicoord2 dms|37|2|24.54|N|137|51|51.21|E|display=ti|region:JP|name=Amatsu Shrine}} |Deities = {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|qid=Q1056017|lt=Amatsuhikohikohononinigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} |Rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor) {{ill|lt=disputed shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}
(claimed) {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q666514|qid=Q666514|1=Echigo Province}} {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} |Founded = (traditional) reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} |Main Hall = {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} copper roofing |Other names = Ichinomiya |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 10|Q2502|qid=Q2502|1=April 10}}・{{ill|lt=October 24|Q2955|qid=Q2955|1=October 24}} |Rites = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Amatsu Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Amatsu Shrine''' (あまつじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ichinomiya, {{ill|lt=Itoigawa City|Q857208|qid=Q857208|1=Itoigawa}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|qid=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=disputed shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} and claims the status of {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of {{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q666514|qid=Q666514|1=Echigo Province}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}. === Overview === It is traditionally said to have been founded during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}, and is also said to have been an imperial prayer shrine (chokuganjo) of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|Q317980|qid=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}. Revered as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q1392097|qid=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}} (tutelary deity) of areas including Ichinomiya, Oshiage, Teramachi, Ōmachi, and Shichikenmachi within the former {{ill|lt=Itoigawa Town|Q11604888|qid=Q11604888|1=Itoigawa}}, it received a donation of 100 koku of {{ill|lt=black-seal land|Q205662|qid=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} from the {{ill|lt=Edo shogunate|Q205662|qid=Q205662|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} in Keichō 16 ({{ill|lt=1611|Q6768|qid=Q6768|1=1611}}), which was changed to red-seal land the following year. In Taishō 7 ({{ill|lt=1918|Q2094|qid=Q2094|1=1918}}) it was ranked as a village shrine, and later promoted to a prefectural shrine. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} named “{{ill|lt=Echigo Province|Q666514|qid=Q666514|1=Echigo Province}} {{ill|lt=Koshi District|Q11664982|qid=Q11664982|1=Kubiki district}} Ōjinja” is listed, and this shrine is considered a candidate site ({{ill|lt=disputed shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}) for that entry. In addition, the subordinate Nunagawa Shrine within the precincts is also a disputed Shikinaisha. === Enshrined deities === * Center: {{ill|lt=Ninigi|Q1056017|qid=Q1056017|lt=Amatsuhikohikohononinigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} * Left: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}} * Right: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Shrine precincts === File:Amatsu-jinja (Itoigawa), honden-1.jpg|Main hall (rear right) and Nunagawa Shrine (left) File:Amatsu-jinja (Itoigawa), honden-2.jpg|Main hall (designated cultural property of Itoigawa City) File:Amatsu-jinja (Itoigawa), emonjo.jpg|Emonjo File:Amatsu-jinja (Itoigawa), torii.jpg|Torii gate === Auxiliary and branch shrines === [[File:Amatsu-jinja (Itoigawa), Nunagawa-jinja.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Nunagawa Shrine}}]] * Nunagawa Shrine *: The principal enshrined deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nunakawa-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, later joined by Yachimata-no-Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}). This area was formerly called “Numakawa-gō,” and {{ill|lt=Japanese mythology|Q814158|qid=Q814158|1=Japanese mythology}} (the {{ill|lt=myths of Ōkuninushi|Q11433656|qid=Q11433656|1=Myth of Ōkuninushi}}) recounts that Yachimata-no-Mikoto visited Nunakawa-hime-no-Mikoto, who lived there. According to tradition, the child born to these two deities was {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391}}, who traveled upstream along the {{ill|lt=Hime River|Q11447417|qid=Q11447417|1=Hime River}} into {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} and developed the land there. *: In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'', it is listed as one of 13 shrines in {{ill|lt=Koshi District|Q11664982|qid=Q11664982|1=Kubiki district}}. There are several shrines named “Nunagawa Shrine” within the district besides this one; the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nunagawa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Tabushi Minami-mura, Itoigawa City (former village shrine), and the {{ill|lt=Nō Hakusan Shrine|Q11611996|qid=Q11611996|lt=Hakusan Shrine|1=Nō Hakusan Shrine}} in Ōaza Nōmiyaue, Itoigawa City (former village shrine), are also considered disputed sites for the Shikinaisha Nunagawa Shrine. File:Amatsu-jinja (Itoigawa), keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the precincts === Festivals === Among its festivals, bugaku (court dance) is well known ({{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine Bugaku|Q11442800|qid=Q11442800|1=Tenshin Shrine Kagura}}). Commonly called “Chigo no Mai” (Dance of the Sacred Children), it is dedicated during the April annual festival. === Cultural properties === === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (National designation) === * Itoigawa and Nō Bugaku — encompassing the {{ill|lt=Amatsu Shrine Bugaku|Q11442800|qid=Q11442800|1=Tenshin Shrine Kagura}} of Itoigawa and the {{ill|lt=Hakusan Shrine Bugaku|Q11579603|qid=Q11579603|1=Hakusan Shrine Bugaku}} of Nō. Designated on January 28, 1980 (Shōwa 55).{{National Cultural Properties Database|302|53|Itoigawa and Nō Bugaku}}
{{National Cultural Properties Database|312|312|Amatsu Shrine Bugaku}} === Niigata Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wooden statue of Nunakawa-hime (sculpture) — Late Heian period. Designated February 10, 1954 (Shōwa 29).{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/130374.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Niigata Prefecture]}} (Niigata Prefecture website). ** Bugaku masks (3 pieces; with 2 additional bugaku masks and 3 chigo dance masks) (sculpture) — Late Kamakura period to late Muromachi period. Designated March 27, 1981 (Shōwa 56). ** Wooden seated statues of goddesses (3 figures) (sculpture) — Heian period to Kamakura period. Designated March 27, 1981 (Shōwa 56). === Itoigawa City Designated Cultural Properties === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Main hall of Amatsu Shrine (structure) — Designated March 26, 1991 (Heisei 3).{{PDFlink|[List of Designated and Registered Cultural Properties]}} (Itoigawa City website). ** Wooden statues of guardian deities of Amatsu Shrine (2 figures) (sculpture) — Designated March 25, 1972 (Shōwa 47). ** Wooden statues of guardian deities of Nunakawa Shrine (2 figures) (sculpture) — Designated March 25, 1972 (Shōwa 47). ** Stone seated statue in the form of a Tathāgata (sculpture) — Designated April 22, 1980 (Shōwa 55). ** Amatsu Shrine large hanging Buddhas (2 pieces) (crafts) — Designated March 26, 1973 (Shōwa 48). === On-site information === '''Address''' * Ichinomiya 1-3-34, {{ill|lt=Itoigawa City|Q857208|qid=Q857208|1=Itoigawa}}, {{ill|lt=Niigata Prefecture|Q132705|qid=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}} '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West) / {{ill|lt=Echigo Tokimeki Railway|Q11260966|qid=Q11260966|1=Echigo Tokimeki Railway}} {{ill|lt=Itoigawa Station|Q871596|qid=Q871596|1=Itoigawa Station}} (about 10 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === See also === * Other shrines claiming the status of Ichinomiya of Echigo Province ** {{ill|lt=Yahiko Shrine|Q195684|qid=Q195684|1=Yahiko Shrine}} ** {{ill|lt=Ita Shrine|Q172417|qid=Q172417|1=Kota Shrine (Niigata)}} === External links === * {{Wayback|url=http://www.fsinet.or.jp/~amatsu/|title=Amatsu Shrine|date=20130313040258}} — Official website {{Shinto navbox}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Amatsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Amatsu Shrine]][[Category:ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Amatsu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Itoigawa]] [[Category:糸魚川市の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]]
